name: First Pace
description_short: First Pace Credit Union is a Minnesota-based financial institution offering savings accounts, money market accounts, and home equity loans with strong financial stability ratings.
description_long:
First Pace Credit Union is a federally-insured credit union headquartered in West St Paul, Minnesota at 161 Marie Ave E. The institution has been evaluated and monitored by MonitorBankRates, an independent rate-tracking platform that aggregates data from over 8,000 financial institutions. As a credit union, First Pace operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution subject to NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) regulatory oversight. The organization has maintained operations in the Twin Cities area and serves the financial needs of its membership base.

First Pace Credit Union offers a range of deposit and lending products typical of community credit unions. Their deposit products include Youth Share Savings accounts (0.25% APY), Share & Club Share Accounts (0.10% APY with $1 minimum balance), Money Market Accounts (0.90% APY with $10,000 minimum), and Christmas Club Accounts (2.00% APY as of the October 2025 dividend declaration). For borrowing, the credit union provides Home Equity loans with rates starting at 6.49% APR, featuring 5-year fixed terms with amortization up to 15 years, LTV up to 80%, and a maximum loan amount of $150,000 with minimal closing costs.

First Pace Credit Union distinguishes itself through exceptional financial health metrics. The institution has earned a 5.0 Star Rating from MonitorBankRates, an A+ Overall Health Grade (99/100 score), and a Texas Ratio of 2.19%—well below the national average of 6.13%. The Texas Ratio measures non-performing assets relative to tangible common equity and loan loss reserves, with lower ratios indicating stronger balance sheets. These metrics demonstrate very low non-performing asset volume and strong capitalization, indicating the credit union maintains a stable and reliable financial position.

First Pace Credit Union represents a solid choice for consumers seeking traditional banking services from a financially stable institution. However, prospective members should note that deposit rates are competitive but not exceptional compared to high-yield online banks (0.25% savings, 0.90% money market). The credit union's service area and membership eligibility criteria are not detailed in available information, and rates are subject to change without notice. Consumers should verify all terms directly with the institution before opening accounts. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share & Club Share Accounts (0.10% APY, $1 minimum)
Christmas Club Accounts (2.00% APY dividend as of 10/1/2025)
5-year fixed home equity rate with up to 15-year amortization
Home equity financing up to 80% LTV
Home equity loans with maximum $150,000 amount
Home equity loans with minimal closing costs
pros: A+ Overall Health Grade with 99/100 financial stability score from independent rating system
5.0 Star Rating from MonitorBankRates based on financial health and member feedback
Texas Ratio of 2.19%, significantly lower than 6.13% national average, indicating strong balance sheet
Home Equity loans with minimal closing costs and flexible terms (5-year fixed, up to 15-year amortization)
Home Equity financing up to 80% LTV with maximum loan amount of $150,000
Multiple savings account options including Christmas Club accounts with 2.00% APY dividend
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal insurance protection
cons: Savings rates are not competitive compared to high-yield online banks (0.25% APY vs. typical 4-5% alternatives)
Money Market Account requires $10,000 minimum balance, limiting access for small savers
Membership eligibility and service area limitations not clearly disclosed in available information
Home Equity APR of 6.49% is not positioned as best-in-market rate
Limited product information provided—no mention of checking accounts, CDs, or loan products beyond home equity
best_for: Credit union members in the West St Paul, Minnesota area seeking stable, federally-insured savings accounts
Homeowners needing home equity loans with flexible terms and minimal closing costs
Consumers prioritizing institutional financial stability and safety over maximum yield rates
diagnosis: First Pace Credit Union is best for existing members or those within its service area in Minnesota seeking safe, stable banking services backed by exceptional financial health ratings. The primary caveat is that deposit rates lag competitive high-yield alternatives, making it less suitable for yield-focused savers, and membership/service area eligibility should be verified directly with the institution.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
